h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: calling it “my reasons” sounds like giving the mechanism a better story
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get the move, almost
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue insisting that reason-responsive control is nothing more than an elegant clockwork mechanism.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the first line is still only an image to me
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that was the problem
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to struggle with finding the right change to notice in the image as prompted by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct was that this scrap of decimals settles it: square root of 2 just keeps going, so it cannot be a fraction.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer the tutor's question about whether reducing a fraction to lowest terms loses any possible fraction equal to the square root of 2.
